message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-016 in .github/dependabot.yml + scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh
What: documented + scripted the lockfile regen Dependabot can't do (an auto-commit workflow was rejected for token/security reasons).
Test(s): test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Automated dependency + transitive-update monitoring (#147). Dependabot opens PRs for
# vulnerable/outdated dependencies on a weekly cadence so the supply-chain surface is tracked,
# not just scanned once. Pairs with the pip-audit gate (reproducible scan of requirements.lock)
# and the CycloneDX SBOM artifact in ci.yml.
#
# NOTE (#G-016): Dependabot bumps the ranges in pyproject.toml but does NOT regenerate the
# hash-pinned uv lockfiles (requirements.lock / requirements-core.lock), so a Dependabot PR will
# FAIL the CI "requirements.lock is stale" gate until a maintainer regenerates them. On a
# Dependabot PR, run `scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh` and commit the result to make it mergeable.
version: 2
updates:
  # Python packages declared in pyproject.toml (and resolved into requirements.lock).
  - package-ecosystem: pip
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: weekly
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    groups:
      # batch low-risk updates so the queue stays reviewable
      minor-and-patch:
        update-types: ["minor", "patch"]
  # The GitHub Actions used by the CI workflows themselves.
  - package-ecosystem: github-actions
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: weekly
===REGEN===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Regenerate the hash-pinned lockfiles from pyproject.toml (#G-016).
#
# Dependabot bumps the version ranges in pyproject.toml but cannot regenerate the uv lockfiles,
# so a Dependabot PR will fail the CI "requirements.lock is stale" gate until the lockfiles are
# regenerated. Run this on a Dependabot branch (or any PR that changes pyproject dependencies),
# then commit the updated requirements*.lock files:
#
#     scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh && git commit -am "chore: regenerate lockfiles"
#
# uv is pinned to the same version CI uses (#F-031) so the output matches the sync check exactly.
set -euo pipefail

UV_VERSION="0.11.21"

if ! command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ "$(uv --version | awk '{print $2}')" != "$UV_VERSION" ]; then
  echo "installing uv==$UV_VERSION (CI-pinned) ..." >&2
  python -m pip install "uv==$UV_VERSION" >&2
fi

uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock
uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock

echo "regenerated requirements.lock + requirements-core.lock — review and commit them."

content.text: Now let me find and read the test.

266	def test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented():
267	    # #G-016: Dependabot can't regenerate the uv lockfiles, so the manual regen path must be
268	    # documented + scripted, or every Dependabot PR is stuck on the stale-lock gate.
269	    regen = _ROOT / "scripts" / "regenerate_lockfiles.sh"
270	    assert regen.exists(), "no scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh (#G-016)"
271	    body = regen.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
272	    assert "uv pip compile" in body and "requirements-core.lock" in body
273	    db = (_ROOT / ".github" / "dependabot.yml").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
274	    assert "regenerate_lockfiles.sh" in db, "dependabot.yml does not point at the regen script"
275	
276	
277	def test_github_actions_are_sha_pinned():
278	    # #F-037: every `uses:` third-party action must be pinned to a 40-char commit SHA (a mutable
279	    # tag can be force-pushed to malicious code). A version tag is allowed only as a comment.
280	    import re
281	
282	    text = _ci_text()
283	    uses = re.findall(r"uses:\s*(\S+)", text)
284	    assert uses, "no actions found"
285	    for ref in uses:
286	        _, _, pin = ref.partition("@")
287	        assert re.fullmatch(r"[0-9a-f]{40}", pin), f"action {ref!r} not pinned to a commit SHA"
288	
289	
290	def test_sbom_is_emitted():
291	    # #147: a CycloneDX SBOM artifact is produced for downstream license/transitive monitoring.
292	    text = _ci_text()
293	    assert "cyclonedx-json" in text and "sbom" in text.lower()
294	
295	
296\[REDACTED](shutil.which("bash") is None, reason="bash required")
297	def test_allowlist_parse_is_newline_agnostic_and_comment_tolerant(tmp_path):
298	    # #131/#145: the last advisory id must be honored even with no trailing newline, comments and
299	    # blank lines skipped, and ids must not word-split/glob-expand.
300	    f = tmp_path / "ignore"
301	    f.write_bytes(b"# a comment\n\nGHSA-aaaa\nPYSEC-2024-last-no-newline")  # no trailing \n
302	    out = subprocess.run(
303	        ["bash", "-c", _PARSE_LOOP, "_", str(f)],
304	        capture_output=True,
305	        text=True,
306	        check=True,
307	    ).stdout
308	    assert "GHSA-aaaa" in out
309	    assert "PYSEC-2024-last-no-newline" in out, "final newline-less id dropped (#131)"
310	    assert "# a comment" not in out, "comment line leaked into ignores (#131)"
311	
312	    # revert-discrimination: the original loop DROPS the final newline-less id.
313	    buggy = subprocess.run(
314	        ["bash", "-c", _PARSE_LOOP_BUGGY, "_", str(f)],
315	        capture_output=True,
316	        text=True,
317	        check=True,
318	    ).stdout
319	    assert "PYSEC-2024-last-no-newline" not in buggy, (
320	        "buggy loop unexpectedly kept the id — test no longer discriminates"
321	    )
322	
323	
324\[REDACTED](shutil.which("bash") is None, reason="bash required")
325	def test_allowlist_ids_do_not_glob_expand(tmp_path):
326	    # #145: an allowlist line is passed as a single argv element even if it contains shell glob
327	    # metacharacters (it must never expand against the working directory).
328	    f = tmp_path / "ignore"
329	    f.write_text("GHSA-*\n", encoding="utf-8")
330	    (tmp_path / "GHSA-real-file").write_text("x", encoding="utf-8")
331	    out = subprocess.run(
332	        ["bash", "-c", f"cd {tmp_path} && " + _PARSE_LOOP, "_", str(f)],
333	        capture_output=True,
334	        text=True,
335	        check=True,
336	    ).stdout
337	    assert "GHSA-*" in out, "glob metacharacter expanded against cwd (#145)"
338	    assert "GHSA-real-file" not in out

content.text: Let me verify the script's compile commands exactly match the CI stale-check commands, and run the test with python3.

content.content:
100	        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
101	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
102	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
103	        run: |
104	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
105	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
106	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
107	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
108	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
109	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
110	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
111	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
112	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
113	          # stay in sync too.
114	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
115	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
116	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
117	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
118	        run: |
119	          set -euo pipefail

content.text:
Let me check the test environment via the project's preferred runner, but the result is already clear enough. Let me confirm the compile commands match exactly between script and CI.

The script uses:
- `uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock`
- `uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock`

CI uses identical commands (lines 108, 114), and uv pinned to the same `0.11.21` (line 107). 

The test couldn't run here due to no pytest in the available interpreters, but I can evaluate its assertions directly against the files, which all hold. Let me confirm there's a venv with pytest.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && ls .venv/bin/pytest 2>/dev/null && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_supply_chain.py::test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented -q 2>&1 | tail -6 || echo "no venv pytest"

content.text:
Test passes. Now my assessment.

**Appropriate:** Both files are correct and idiomatic. The script uses `set -euo pipefail`, pins uv to `0.11.21` (matching CI's `#F-031` pin exactly), and its two `uv pip compile` invocations are byte-for-byte identical to the CI stale-lock gate commands (ci.yml lines 108/114), so running it will genuinely satisfy the gate. Script is executable (`-rwxr-xr-x`) and passes `bash -n`. The dependabot.yml documents the limitation in a clear NOTE pointing maintainers at the script, and is valid YAML. No regression — these are additive supply-chain files.

**Complete:** The fix fully closes G-016 — it provides both the documentation (dependabot.yml NOTE) and the script Dependabot itself cannot run. This is a doc/tooling item, so revert-discrimination is judged by coverage rather than a buggy-variant assertion. The test discriminates structurally on the essentials:
- `regen.exists()` — fails if the script is deleted (reverted)
- `"uv pip compile" in body and "requirements-core.lock" in body` — fails if the script loses its compile logic
- `"regenerate_lockfiles.sh" in db` — fails if dependabot.yml stops pointing at the script

If the fix were reverted (script removed, NOTE removed), the test fails on line 270 or 274. The named test exists, passes, and gates the exact artifacts the fix introduces.

One minor observation (not a CONCERN): the test asserts the script *mentions* the right command strings but doesn't verify the flag-level equivalence with CI (e.g. `--all-extras`/`--universal`/`--generate-hashes`); a typo in those flags would pass the test but produce a stale-failing lockfile. However, for a doc/tooling item this level of coverage is reasonable, and I manually confirmed the commands match CI exactly. Verdict stands as CONFIRMED.

input.reasoning:
Appropriate: scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh is correct and idiomatic — set -euo pipefail, uv pinned to 0.11.21 matching CI's #F-031 pin, and its two `uv pip compile` invocations are byte-identical to the CI stale-lock gate (ci.yml lines 108/114: --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes for requirements.lock; --extra dev for requirements-core.lock). Script is executable and passes `bash -n`. .github/dependabot.yml is valid and adds a clear NOTE pointing maintainers at the script. Additive supply-chain files — no regression.

Complete: This is a doc/tooling item (an auto-commit workflow was rejected for token/security reasons), so it is judged by coverage rather than revert-discrimination of a code path. The fix provides both halves of what G-016 needs — the documentation (dependabot.yml NOTE) and the script Dependabot cannot run itself. tests/test_supply_chain.py::test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented passes (.venv pytest: 1 passed) and discriminates structurally: it fails if the script is deleted (line 270 regen.exists()), if the script loses its compile logic (line 272 'uv pip compile' / 'requirements-core.lock'), or if dependabot.yml stops referencing the script (line 274). Reverting the fix breaks the test.

Minor non-blocking note: the test checks command substrings but not flag-level equivalence with CI (a typo in --all-extras/--universal/--generate-hashes would still pass the test yet produce a stale-failing lockfile). For a doc/tooling item this coverage is acceptable, and I manually verified the script commands match the CI gate exactly.

content.text: Verdict: CONFIRMED. The G-016 fix is both appropriate and complete.
